sprint: Story Egg Hatching / Lumo Ceremony Visibility Fix
task: Fixes a real, user-reported bug: "when we click on publish from studio the egg disappears why? the flow of egg hatching and lumo assisting in hatching is missing." Audited directly against docs/COMPANION_CANON.md's own Canon 1/Canon 3 (the Story Egg's canonical 'hatching' pose, and the 'Lumo Ceremony' stage of the Creator Lifecycle) and found a genuine, confirmed gap between what the Canon documents and what a Story Author ever actually saw. Root cause: Sprint C1's own dialog-occlusion watcher (js/companionDirector.js's BUSY_SELECTORS) predates the Magic Card Awakening ceremony and treated #magicCardOverlay as just another dialog to hide behind -- but the ceremony always opens on top of an already-open .publish-studio-modal (itself a BUSY_SELECTOR, open for the whole Read/Almost Ready/Destination/Publishing flow), so the companion widget was hidden the instant Publish Studio opened and never reappeared until the whole flow closed. Two already-correct, already-firing moments were consequently invisible: notify('published') setting the Story Egg's canonical 'hatching' pose, and notify('creator-born')'s live Egg-to-Lumo swap mid-ceremony. Fix: #magicCardOverlay:not(.hidden) is now a separate CEREMONY_SELECTOR checked first in isStudioBusy(), forcing the companion visible whenever the Magic Card overlay is open regardless of what else (Publish Studio) is open behind it; css/style.css's .companion-widget z-index raised from 1200 to 1450 so it can actually paint through the overlay's own full-bleed opaque background (1400) rather than being covered by it, while staying below the restore-session modal (2000). No architecture change, no new choreography, no new assets -- both notify() calls were already correct, they simply had nowhere visible to render. Verified via a new Playwright suite: companion stays hidden during Read/Destination as before; becomes visible for the whole Awakening reveal + claim panel; on Claim, visibly swaps to Lumo (wave pose, real greeting bubble) beside the First Claimed Moment's two-sided card, genuinely realizing 'Lumo assisting'; on Maybe Later, settles to idle and correctly hides again once only Publish Studio remains open, reappearing once Publish Studio itself closes. Full regression across the existing Companion/Magic Card/Creator Playwright suite and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged; a handful of already-stale, pre-existing test failures (predating this fix, unrelated to it) were confirmed via git stash to already fail identically on the unmodified baseline. Confined to js/companionDirector.js and css/style.css.
